title: Geometric part: i_K^* must commute with pi_j for layer geometricity, and Postnikov assembly (a limit) needs convergence to land in tau

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap: Postnikov-tower assembly of the geometric part conflates spectrum-level and layer-level geometricity, and treats a limit as a colimit

This refines the concern in q5-8, isolating two distinct unproved steps in `lem:geompart` and its use in the converse.

**(A) From $i_K^*X\simeq 0$ to "each homotopy Mackey functor is geometric".**
The solution proves $i_K^*(\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E)\simeq 0$ for proper $K$ (spectrum level) and then declares each $\underline\pi_j(\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E)$ "geometric" in the sense of Definition `def:geom`, which demands $i_K^*\,H\underline\pi_j\simeq 0$. The passage from the spectrum vanishing to the *layer's EM-spectrum* vanishing requires the (true but unstated) fact that $i_K^*$ commutes with the genuine homotopy Mackey functors:
$$ i_K^*\,\underline\pi_j(X)\ =\ \underline\pi_j(i_K^*X), $$
so that $i_K^*X\simeq0\Rightarrow i_K^*\underline\pi_j(X)=0\Rightarrow i_K^*H\underline\pi_j(X)\simeq0$. This commutation (exactness/colimit-preservation of $i_K^*$ on $t$-structure hearts) is never invoked, yet it is exactly what makes Definition `def:geom`'s hypothesis hold for the layers.

**(B) A Postnikov tower is a limit; closure of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ under it is not automatic.**
`lem:geompart` concludes $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge c[G:c]}$ by asserting it "is built by a finite/transfinite sequence of cofiber sequences out of these layers", using closure of $\tau$ under extensions and homotopy colimits. But the Postnikov tower $\{\tau_{\le j}X\}$ is a tower under **limits**, and $X\to\varprojlim$ being an equivalence (convergence) plus identifying $X$ with a **colimit** of objects built from the layers is precisely the bounded-below convergence statement that is not established here (it is the same gap flagged for the main tower in q5-14). A localizing subcategory is closed under colimits and extensions, *not* under arbitrary limits; so one must explicitly present $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes E$ as a sequential colimit of finite extensions of the layers $\Sigma^j H\underline\pi_j$ ($j\ge c$) and invoke convergence. As written, the membership conclusion does not follow from the stated closure properties.

Both (A) and (B) are standard once made explicit, but each is currently a genuine logical gap in the chain that delivers the geometric half of the converse.
